items.title: Testing for ‘Bad Cholesterol’ Doesn’t Tell the Whole Story
items.summary: There’s a more accurate way of measuring who’s at risk for cholesterol-related health issues. So why don’t more doctors use it?

items.title: The FCC Received Hundreds of Complaints About Bad Bunny’s ‘Vulgar’ Super Bowl Performance
items.summary: The complaints, obtained by WIRED, described Bad Bunny’s performance as being overly sexual and protested that the show was in Spanish.

items.summary: Google had urged Apple for years to support RCS texting to make communication between each company's devices more seamless.

items.title: Daniel Ek-backed defense tech Helsing to raise $1.2B at $18B valuation
items.summary: Five-year old European military drone startup Helsing is close to raising another monster round.

items.title: Bravo is creating unscripted microdramas for the Peacock app
items.summary: As microdrama apps like ReelShort and DramaBox quietly rake in billions, Peacock announced on Monday that it is launching two unscripted Bravo microdramas, which will stream in the Peacock app.

items.title: Last passengers leave virus-hit cruise ship as three more test positive
items.summary: An American and a French national who have returned home have tested positive for hantavirus.

items.title: US passengers quarantining in Nebraska as officials say public hantavirus risk 'very low'
items.summary: Two people are being monitored in Atlanta, 16 in Nebraska and another six passengers in Canada.

items.title: South African president says he will not step down after impeachment call
items.summary: President Cyril Ramaphosa said he would not resign on Monday evening after days of speculation.

items.title: Series of court decisions weaken Democrats' odds of winning the House
items.summary: A U.S. Supreme Court decision and ruling from the Virginia Supreme Court have altered the redistricting outlook, hurting the chance that House control will flip.

items.title: JPMorgan Chase-led bank group reins in credit line to troubled KKR private credit fund as losses mount
items.summary: The KKR fund, often referred to by its ticker, FSK, has become one of the most visible fault lines in the private credit story.

items.title: Michael Burry is not a believer: 'For any stocks going parabolic reduce positions almost entirely'
items.summary: Michael Burry said investors should "reject greed" as enthusiasm around artificial intelligence and momentum-driven trades pushes valuations sharply higher.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.summary: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.title: Supreme Court lifts block on Alabama Republicans’ map
items.summary: The Supreme Court on Monday wiped out a decision blocking Alabama Republicans’ congressional map, potentially allowing it to be used for the midterms in the wake of the justices’ recent blockbuster ruling narrowing the Voting Rights Act. The design would remove one of Alabama’s two majority-Black districts and give Republicans a better shot at winning the seat held by Democratic…

items.title: Byron Allen taking over BuzzFeed
items.summary: Byron Allen is expected to head BuzzFeed as the company’s new CEO, according to a Monday company release. Allen will replace Founder and CEO Jonah Peretti, who will transition into the role of president of BuzzFeed AI. Allen’s investment firm, Allen Family Digital, purchased 40 million shares of BuzzFeed Inc. at a price of $3.00…

items.title: Hantavirus outbreak: Who is most at risk of catching the deadly virus?
items.summary: While hantavirus respiratory infections may still be rare in the U.S., they can be incredibly deadly.

items.title: Scientists say this common sweetener may be quietly rewiring your metabolism
items.summary: Researchers say fructose is not just “empty calories” — it may actively push the body toward fat storage and metabolic disease. A new review found that fructose affects the body differently from glucose, disrupting normal energy regulation and promoting processes linked to obesity, insulin resistance, and cardiovascular problems.

items.title: This 800-year-old Chinese exercise helps lower blood pressure naturally
items.summary: An ancient Chinese exercise routine may be just as powerful as a daily brisk walk for lowering blood pressure — without equipment, gyms, or intense workouts. In a major clinical trial, adults with stage 1 hypertension who practiced baduanjin, a gentle mind-body exercise combining slow movements, breathing, and meditation, saw meaningful drops in blood pressure within three months that lasted for an entire year.

items.title: NASA’s Psyche probe is about to slingshot around Mars at 12,000 mph
items.summary: NASA’s Psyche spacecraft is about to pull off a dramatic close flyby of Mars, skimming just 2,800 miles above the planet to get a powerful gravitational boost on its journey to the mysterious metal-rich asteroid Psyche. The maneuver will save propellant while giving mission scientists a rare chance to test and calibrate the spacecraft’s instruments using Mars as a target. As Psyche approaches from the planet’s dark side, it’s expected to capture striking crescent views of Mars, search for faint dust rings around the planet, and even gather magnetic and cosmic ray data during the encounter.

items.title: Astronomers may have found a record-breaking pair of black holes
items.summary: At some 60 billion times the mass of the sun, this dark void could be home to a pair of black holes that are due for a cosmic collision.

items.title: Some South American rodent-borne viruses may spread as climate warms
items.summary: Some rodents in South America carry arenaviruses and hantaviruses. Climate change may bring both to regions where neither is currently a threat.

items.title: Yawning is contagious — even in the womb
items.summary: Rather than catching a yawn on sight, muscles squeezing the uterus could be the trigger for a fetus to catch a yawn from its mother.
